FluentGpu
Table of Contents

Struct StateBrush

Namespace
FluentGpu.Controls
Assembly
FluentGpu.Controls.dll

Reusable, WinUI-accurate discrete-state motion presets for control sub-elements — the checkmarks, dots and glyphs that appear/disappear on a logical state flip (checked, selected, expanded…). These ride the engine's existing enter/exit lifecycle: attach one as Animate on a node the reconciler INSERTS on enter and REMOVES on exit, and it scales+fades in (on insert) / out (orphaned, then reclaimed when the spring settles, on removal). The motion is property-level and signals-native — the state IS the element's presence and the animation falls out of the reconcile diff — so there is no per-control animation code and no VisualStateManager: the opposite of WinUI's stringly-typed GoToState storyboards, and a strict superset in composability. Springs (velocity-carrying, interruptible) are the default; durations map to the Fluent motion tokens.

public readonly record struct StateBrush : IEquatable<StateBrush>
Implements
IEquatable<StateBrush>
Inherited Members
ValueType.Equals(object)

ValueType.ToString()

Constructors

StateBrush(ColorF, ColorF, ColorF, ColorF)

Reusable, WinUI-accurate discrete-state motion presets for control sub-elements — the checkmarks, dots and glyphs that appear/disappear on a logical state flip (checked, selected, expanded…). These ride the engine's existing enter/exit lifecycle: attach one as Animate on a node the reconciler INSERTS on enter and REMOVES on exit, and it scales+fades in (on insert) / out (orphaned, then reclaimed when the spring settles, on removal). The motion is property-level and signals-native — the state IS the element's presence and the animation falls out of the reconcile diff — so there is no per-control animation code and no VisualStateManager: the opposite of WinUI's stringly-typed GoToState storyboards, and a strict superset in composability. Springs (velocity-carrying, interruptible) are the default; durations map to the Fluent motion tokens.

Methods

Flat(ColorF)

A flat ramp (all four the same) — for a property that doesn't react to interaction.

public static StateBrush Flat(ColorF c)

Parameters

c ColorF

Returns

StateBrush

Resting(bool)

The resting value for the current enablement (the hover/pressed legs are eased by the engine).

public ColorF Resting(bool enabled)

Parameters

enabled bool

Returns

ColorF
FluentGpu — a near-zero-allocation, NativeAOT, GPU-rendered UI engine for .NET 10. Usage docs live here; architecture canon lives in design/ (SPEC-INDEX.md is the precedence authority).
